context: You lately have been evicted from your house and you and your family of 3 have nowhere to go. You found a house, affordable for your family and manageable, but the price of the deposit is high: you need 30 000$ for the deposit and for two months rent.

conversation_summaries.overall_pre_game:
I felt most connected to F — we made a concrete "bluebird" pact to watch each other's backs, split late and share intel 50/50 — that felt like a real partnership. D also felt close because we agreed on clear signals (tap twice before any big play) and committed to warning each other about risky moves. I trusted I as well; our two‑knock and whisper signal felt like a dependable way to coordinate and call out suspicions. I felt least connected to J — they were friendly and supportive, but we never made plans or concrete signals.

Yes — I made several specific agreements: "bluebird" with F for a late, split grab, tap‑twice with D before big plays, and two knocks/whisper with I to signal problems. Going in I trust F, D, and I most because of those concrete signals and plans; I don’t really trust J (or anyone I haven't set signals with) the same way until they prove themselves.
